item.content:encoded: Thank you rounder.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gotta respect all the "House" jokes that came with this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So… the game was rigged from the start?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fun fact: Holy water, at least in the Catholic tradition (or so it was explained to me), is easy to make. Start with your container of choice, add greater than 50% of the container's volume in holy water, top off with clean, uncontaminated secular water, and now you have a container of 100% holy water. Repeat as necessary, to produce exponentially increasing quantities of holy water. Your reserves should never run out!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I absolutely love this. It's like a metropolitan, corrupted version of Sloth's Pit. Absolutely spectacular. +1

item.title: great
item.content:encoded: +1 and also, im surprised i didn't see a holy hand grenade

item.title: Name Correction-
item.content:encoded: When Pluto is talking about the casinos, it mentions New New York instead of New York New York.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought "Las Vegas is literally overrun by hell" would be too cheesy and stale, but the narrative flow works and keeps the whole thing entertaining.

+1 for disrupting the joke of betting your soul on an unorthodox game, and also for choosing Pluto over Hades

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Omega thirty-three "Ocean's Three-hundred"

I love you.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad you liked it, and thank you for translating it!

item.title: Re: Minor Typo
item.content:encoded: Good catch, thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Never been one for suspension of disbelief myself.

item.title: Minor Typo
item.content:encoded: In Addendum 4661.3 it says Scantron instead of Scranton.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a relatively well-put-together skip that had its charming moments and easily high-effort. However, there are a few things that are preventing me from upvoting:

  • The TRE stuff is never really explained and is dropped almost immediately after being introduced. It feels like it could be cut out of the skip at no cost. I think I'm paying special attention to this because it feels like there should be some explanation of TRE that never really comes.
  • The dialogue before the Pluto confrontation feels stilted and overly casual. It feels like it's just meant to be a means to an end to build up to the confrontation.
  • There's a lot of conceptual and structural overlap with SCP-3667 that bothered me throughout.
  • The ending felt underwhelming and cliche. It feels like you could've ending the skip without the email and it would be a better ending.
  • My suspension of disbelief was shattered from the first addendum. How does the Foundation expect to cover this up? (Unless I missed some cue that this is Broken Masquerade or something like that). It's never really addressed at any point, unless I missed something. In addition, even if they were carpet-bombing the area with amnestics, I live within a relatively close distance to Las Vegas. I feel like I would notice if something like this started happening.

Novote.

item.title: Re: Military photos
item.content:encoded: The average reader does not care about if every little detail in a photo is accurate.

item.title: Military photos
item.content:encoded:
There are a few problems with the military photos. Not going to nitpick every piece of gear on them but these ones really jump out at me:

Addendum 4661.3 The camo pattern on "MTF Omega-18 "Patriots" did not exist in 1992. It is the Army Combat Uniform Universal Camouflage Pattern. From Wikipedia:
"The pattern was chosen after laboratory and field tests from 2003 to 2004 showed it to provide the best concealment in many different operational environments." Camo patterns in the 90s looked different.

Addendum 4661.5 The guys in this photo have blank adapters clearly visible on their weapons.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually $10,000 in 1992 is about $16,661.72 now, so he could get a new car. A 2019 Toyota Yaris L Sedan is would leave him with about $300.

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: gaminfish, try to keep discussion on other authors' works focused on the work in question, rather than using the discussion to talk about your own writing. The forums or IRC chatrooms would be the place to seek help with any difficulties you're having with the site's writing process.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I cant find any scp ideas that interest people like this

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
An excelent work, I enjoyed it a lot.

…and sometimes a rigged game is justifiable

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If House’s card is a six and he receives a three on the hit, it should be impossible for him to bust, no?

House was showing a six, but had a 4 face down. So with the 3 that makes 13 total, and if he hit the jack instead of the five he would've bust at 23.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why does Hell have use for American currency, and why is dominion over a large region of unchecked iniquity worth about as much as a used car?

Well they're blending in to Vegas so I'd assume they'd be using American money, hence the dealer would want American money.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Damn he was one year off.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Concerns:

If House’s card is a six and he receives a three on the hit, it should be impossible for him to bust, no?

Why does Hell have use for American currency, and why is dominion over a large region of unchecked iniquity worth about as much as a used car?

Why would the protagonist forget the order in which the deck was stacked when only the position of a single card is relevant - and for that matter, what stacking methodology would be used to force the Nth card to be dealt?

I like the overall feel to this article, but I can’t bring myself to vote for it. My primary concern is the methodology of the rigged game, which lacks the subterfuge or execution that I would expect. No mention of counting cards, no attempt by the Devil to manipulate the game as well, which is subsequently outplayed (the lack of implied cheating on the Devil’s part when his opponent has amateurishly done so rankles), no bare-minimum Foundation cautionary procedures to discern whether the deck has been tampered with, et cetera. I understand the need to keep the betting round to a single hand for brevity’s sake, but there are definitely ways that you could improve here. Tentative -1, but looking forward to flipping it upside-downways.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Whole hearted agreed definitely deserves more +1's.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
From where Pluto's sitting it must seem like an 18-carat run of bad luck.
Truth is… The game was rigged from the start.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Absolutely blasphemous.

Wait, isn't that the point?

+1. Almost -1'd for insulting Mtg AND making a terrible pun in the same article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't base it on any one particular skip — I've just seen the term tossed around a lot in articles until it kind of just became the logical thing to use. Glad you liked the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Real glad you liked it, Tufts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! I pride myself on using formatting well, so it's nice to hear it does some good for people besides me, lol.

item.title: Re: Provisional Licensing Information
item.content:encoded: Noting that the rest of the images have been sourced and are cc-compliant

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 😉

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A fun piece overall, even though I found the dialogue with Pluto to be a little cringey. It's a good story without being too long.

Did you use the "tartarean" terminology based on SCP-2336?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I haven't seen such a good article in days!

And I kind of feel proud of myself for staying at Site-616 in the past. I mean, the "1" wasn't a 6, so that's a plus.

item.title: Provisional Licensing Information

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great skip! Could honestly pass as a Supernatural episode.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I kept wondering when you'd put a "The house always wins" joke in this, just when I thought we were safe you stick it in the stinger :p.

This is a cracking skip, that really understands how to make something like this work: backing the wacky idea up with a tone that complements it. You get how to make wacky Vegas hijinks amenable to the audience extremely well, and all the scenes with meetings understand that sleazy, comedy backstabbing that one must have in a skip like that extremely well.

Easy +1, very well done indeed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I quite enjoy how the formatting is used to demonstrate the evolution of the situation. It does an excellent job of enhancing the flow of the story. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What happens in Vegas…

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wish I could upvote this twice. Well done.

+1

The House always wins.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If my knowledge of the Fourth Circle of Hell as reported by Inferno, there should be a few people jousting with heavy rolling objects. Either way,

HOUSE: I put the bribe money under your name. You have a big budget but I'd thought you'd like to know.
THOMSON: Go to hell.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seymour

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
With all that in mind, there's really only one rule in Undervegas: the House always wins.

Fucking Christ, just take your updoot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-4661 is a region of space wherein the city of Las Vegas and the Fourth Circle of Hell (as reported in Dante Alighieri's Inferno) overlap

Did it not before? +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was a devilishly good time. Easy +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Delightfully devilish

ive fucking BEEN to the triangle casino wtf

also I hate Vegas I can’t imagine having to be in that site it would suck and probably cut off a few of your years of life cause of all the phucking smo King and drin King

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I suggest D&D. Churches used to burn that stuff for being demonic. Bonus: It's not a real game, either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait what? Neither did MTG.

item.title: GNU Terry Pratchett
item.content:encoded:
“Over the centuries, mankind has tried many ways of combating the forces of evil… prayer, fasting, good works and so on. Up until Doom, no one seemed to have thought about the double-barrel shotgun."

—Terry Pratchett

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ah, the Department of Applied Force is from Djkaktus' TWIE — essentially the Foundation's military department.

And fun fact, that MtG line originally said Yu Gi Oh before I learned Yu Gi Oh didn't exist in 1992!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Department of Applied Force

The what, now?

HOUSE: Magic the Gathering.

PLUTO: What? No. Pick a real fucking game.

How dare you come into my house and attack me like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: [satanic reference]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I knew the pun was coming as soon as I saw House's name, and I still groaned.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Glad to see you posted this.

I've still got some gripes with the primary description and the overuse of "they", and the escalation of conflict in the addendum still needs a climax before the show-down. I still can't quite get behind the "House" character, or "Pluto" for that matter.

But it's a fun read, and a very cute concept. I'd just like to have seen more grounded events and responses. Right now, it feels as though "Vegas slipping into hell" is happening in a bit of a vaccum. Sure, there's some lethargy, horned folks and general hedonism, but it doesn't seem all that… dangerous?

+1 still. Good work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reminds me of Good Omens.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I dig this. The main sore point for me is Hades as a character; I’ve seen variations of “god updated for 21st century is a cheerful smooth talker angry at another, unseen important religious figure” too many times to do more than roll my eyes when he first showed up. On the flip side, the blackjack game was fun to read and I was enthusiastically pleased to discover that the game had been rigged.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How is this not getting more +1's quicker? This is amazing!
